description: A pasta dish of spaghetti served with a meat-based tomato sauce. Outside Italy it is widely known as spaghetti Bolognese, though traditional ragù alla bolognese is usually served with broader pasta rather than spaghetti.
preparation_steps: Cook the spaghetti until just tender and drain.
Sweat onion, carrot, and celery in oil until softened.
Brown the meat and cook off excess moisture.
Add tomatoes and any optional wine or seasonings, then simmer until the sauce thickens and develops flavor.
Serve the sauce over the pasta and finish with cheese if desired.

health_advice.benefits: Provides carbohydrates for energy and protein from the meat sauce.
health_advice.risks: Can be high in sodium and saturated fat depending on the meat, cheese, and portion size.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use lean meat, add extra vegetables, and keep cheese and salt moderate.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional versions are meat-based. Vegetarian or vegan versions can use lentils, mushrooms, or plant mince. Gluten-free suitability depends on the pasta used.

allergens: Milk if cheese or milk is used
common_variations: Spaghetti with extra herbs or chili
